title: Man tased by Syracuse police in convenience store arrest; they say he sold drugs outside
summary: Syracuse police arrested Tashawn Albert on April 9, 2025 at a Park Street convenience store for alleged drug possession and resisting arrest, deploying a taser during the encounter. Albert has filed a complaint with the Civilian Review Board alleging excessive force, claiming he suffered spinal fracture, broken tooth, and taser injuries, while police assert Albert resisted and reached toward his waistband. The incident highlights tensions between law enforcement procedures and civilian oversight mechanisms.

political_signal.description: Public complaint filed against police use of force; civilian review board engagement suggests constituent pressure for accountability and transparency in law enforcement conduct.
